Suppress Microsoft.AspNetCore INF logs for dashboard/internal endpoints (#19)

* Suppress Microsoft.AspNetCore INF logs for dashboard/internal endpoints

- Register IHttpContextAccessor so the Serilog filter can inspect the
  current request path at log-emit time.
- Switch Serilog setup to the service-aware UseSerilog overload which
  provides access to the built DI container.
- Add Filter.ByExcluding that suppresses Microsoft.AspNetCore
  Information-and-below log events (Request starting/finished, route
  matched, executing action/endpoint, etc.) whenever the current request
  path is one of the internal/dashboard paths already excluded by
  RequestLoggingMiddleware.IsExcludedPath.
- Make RequestLoggingMiddleware.IsExcludedPath internal so Program.cs
  can reuse it — single source of truth for the excluded path list.

Co-authored-by: Lakerfield <1453606+Lakerfield@users.noreply.github.com>

Roadmap infrastructure: storage, API, agent tools

Storage:
- Roadmap/RoadmapStep types in storage.ts
- getSessionRoadmap/setSessionRoadmap — persists to roadmap.json

API:
- GET /api/sessions/:id/roadmap — returns roadmap or {}
- PATCH /api/sessions/:id/roadmap — merges updates

Agent tools:
- get_roadmap — reads current roadmap (essential after compaction)
- update_roadmap — structured actions: set_title, add/update/remove_step,
  add/remove_document, reorder_steps. Atomic operations prevent JSON corruption.

process: fix role gaps — integration gate, spec validation, smoke test ceremonies

Root cause analysis from M1+M2 found 5 blockers reached code review
because integration, spec validation, and smoke testing were assumed
but unassigned. This commit fixes the process gaps:

Ceremonies added:
- integration-gate: hard gate after every parallel wave (Solo owns)
- spec-validation: GDD compliance check before PR merge (Jango owns)
- godot-smoke-test: end-to-end verification after milestones (Ackbar owns)

Routing updated:
- Integration verification routed to Solo
- Spec compliance routed to Jango during PR review
- Post-merge smoke test routed to Ackbar
- Branch management routed to Mace

Charters updated:
- Jango: GDD spec compliance in every PR review
- Solo: integration gatekeeper after each wave
- Ackbar: post-milestone smoke test owner
- Mace: branch management and base validation

New skill created:

feat: make OTP login passwordless (email-only) (#64)

## Summary

Makes the "Login with email code" flow truly passwordless.

### Changes
- `app/schemas/auth.py` — `LoginOTPRequest` is now its own schema with
only `email` (previously an alias of `LoginRequest` which required a
password)
- `app/api/routes/authentication/login_otp.py` — removed password
validation from `POST /auth/login/otp/request`; now only checks the
account exists, is verified, and not banned before sending the OTP code

### Before
Users had to provide **email + password** to request an OTP code.

### After
Users only need their **email** — the OTP code sent to their inbox is
the sole authentication factor.

commit.message:
feat(sharp): replace noisy per-file logs with ETA progress reporting

The old code logged 'Processed X/total' every 10 files using the file's
array index rather than the actual completion count. With concurrent
processing via p-limit the index bears no relation to completion order,
producing out-of-order, misleading output at scale (9000+ assets).

Changes:
- Remove index/total parameters from optimizeImageFile; move all
  progress tracking into the limit() wrapper in compress() where the
  completion count is accurate and atomic.
- CI mode: print a [sharp]-prefixed log line on the first completion,
  at every 5% milestone, after any 60s silence, and on completion.
  Format: '[sharp] done/total (pct%) – rate files/s – elapsed – ETA'
- Interactive mode: overwrite a single terminal line with \r, showing
  a 30-char block progress bar, completion count, percentage, throughput,
  and a live countdown ETA. Writes \n once all files are done.
- ETA uses a simple rolling average (elapsed/done * remaining) which
  converges quickly for a uniform workload like image compression.

fix: resolve 4 CodeQL alerts — shell injection, use-after-lifetime, gmtime

- IccAnalyzerReport.cpp: Replace popen(sha256sum) with OpenSSL EVP_sha256()
  file-based hash (eliminates CWE-78 shell injection). Replace gmtime()
  with gmtime_r() (thread-safe, resolves cpp/potentially-dangerous-function).
- IccAnalyzerXMLExport.cpp: Replace popen(sha256sum) with OpenSSL EVP_sha256()
  file-based hash (eliminates CWE-78 shell injection via icc/xml-all-attacks).
- IccHeuristicsDataValidation.cpp: Remove reference binding to
  CIccTagProfileSequenceId — iterate via pointer directly
  (resolves 3× cpp/use-after-expired-lifetime alerts).

Build: 217/217 tests pass. 0 ASAN/UBSAN in spot-check.

commit.message:
feat: initial commit of data-service-student-riskengine

Kafka Streams microservice that monitors student attendance
and computes risk levels (LOW/MEDIUM/HIGH) using a 30-day
tumbling window aggregation.

Topology:
  A. Profile KTable    — consumes student_profiles, materializes STUDENT-PROFILES-MV
  B. Attendance KTable — 30-day windowed agg, materializes ATTENDANCE-FEATURES-MV
  C. Risk State Join   — left-join profiles x features -> student_risk_state
  D. Risk Alerts       — stateful transition detection -> student_risk_alerts

Includes:
  - Full Avro schemas for all 5 domain types
  - 72 unit + topology integration tests (100% passing)
  - SonarCloud integration via SONAR_TOKEN secret
  - GitHub Actions: build, sonar, release workflows
  - CODEOWNERS, CONTRIBUTING.md, commit-msg-template
  - README with ASCII topology diagram and schema reference

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
Fix: store shot coordinates as raw tap positions

Previously, period 2 shots had their coordinates inverted during
recording (x = 1-x, y = 1-y) because of a flipped-rink coordinate
transform. But the rink SVG never actually rotated visually, so the
coach was tapping based on the un-rotated rink while coordinates
were being silently flipped. This caused P2 shots to appear on the
wrong side of the rink in the summary report.

Shots are now stored exactly where the coach taps. The rink is a
fixed map of the ice surface, and coordinates represent physical
locations regardless of which period they were recorded in. The
direction labels below the rink still flip between periods to help
the coach orient themselves.

Keep ensure_block_ids in save_current as safety net

Intentional duplication: autosave_if_dirty (hot path, every
keystroke) and save_current (direct calls, session save) both
call ensure_block_ids. It's a no-op when IDs are already
assigned, but ensures no save path can write content without IDs.
